title: Laptop Battery Not Charging
cardText: Diagnose a laptop battery that is not charging correctly.
Check the charger, power source, battery status, operating system settings, and battery health.
Separate charger faults, battery faults, and laptop hardware faults.
text: This guide is for laptops that show charging problems.
The battery may not charge, may charge slowly, or may remain at a fixed percentage.
Check the charger and power source before investigating software or hardware issues.
Escalate hardware faults when basic troubleshooting does not resolve the problem.

checkChargerConnected.title: Check charger connection
body: Ensure the charger is firmly connected to both the laptop and power outlet.
Check for loose connections or partially inserted plugs.
Many charging faults are caused by simple connection issues.

reconnectCharger.title: Reconnect charger
body: Reconnect the charger securely.
Verify the connection at both ends.
Check whether charging resumes.

checkChargingIndicator.title: Check charging indicator
body: Look for charging lights or battery charging symbols.
Most laptops show visual indicators when external power is detected.
This helps determine whether the charger is being recognized.

checkPowerOutlet.title: Check power outlet
body: Test the outlet with another device.
If using a power board, ensure it is switched on.
Try another wall outlet if available.

restorePower.title: Restore power source
body: The outlet is not supplying power.
Restore power or use another outlet.
Retest charging afterwards.

inspectCharger.title: Inspect charger
body: Check the charger cable, connector, and adapter brick.
Look for frayed cables, bent connectors, burn marks, or physical damage.
Damaged chargers often fail intermittently before failing completely.

replaceCharger.title: Replace charger
body: The charger appears damaged.
Replace it with a compatible charger.
Retest charging after replacement.

testAlternateCharger.title: Test another charger
body: If available, test with a known-good compatible charger.
This helps separate charger faults from laptop faults.
Use only compatible chargers.

chargerFaultFound.title: Original charger faulty
body: The laptop charges with another charger.
Replace the original charger.
Verify normal charging operation afterwards.

checkBatteryPercentage.title: Check battery percentage
body: Observe whether the battery percentage increases while connected to power.
Leave the charger connected for several minutes.
A charging icon does not always mean the battery is actually charging.

chargingWorking.title: Battery charging normally
body: The battery percentage is increasing.
Charging appears to be functioning correctly.
No further action is required.

checkWindowsBatteryStatus.title: Check operating system battery status
body: Look at the battery icon and power settings.
Check for messages such as Plugged In Not Charging.
These messages can indicate battery health or charging control issues.

batteryManagementCheck.title: Check battery management software
body: Some manufacturers limit charging to preserve battery lifespan.
Look for battery conservation or battery health settings.
These settings can intentionally stop charging at a certain percentage.

disableBatteryLimit.title: Adjust battery management settings
body: Battery conservation settings are limiting charging.
Adjust the settings if appropriate.
Retest charging afterwards.

powerReset.title: Perform power reset
body: Shut down the laptop completely.
Disconnect power and hold the power button for 15–30 seconds.
Reconnect power and test charging again.

checkBatteryHealth.title: Check battery health
body: Review battery health information if available.
Older batteries gradually lose charging capacity.
A severely degraded battery may stop charging normally.

replaceBattery.title: Replace battery
body: The battery appears to have reached the end of its useful life.
Replace it with a compatible battery.
Verify charging after replacement.

checkFirmware.title: Check BIOS and firmware updates
body: Some charging problems are caused by firmware issues.
Check for manufacturer BIOS, EC, or firmware updates.
Apply updates according to manufacturer guidance.

updateFirmware.title: Update firmware
body: Install available firmware updates.
Restart the laptop and retest charging.
Firmware updates can resolve battery communication issues.

hardwareFault.title: Hardware diagnostics required
body: The charger, battery, power source, and software checks did not resolve the issue.
Possible causes include charging circuitry faults, motherboard faults, damaged charging ports, or battery communication failures.
Escalate for hardware repair or replacement.
